corelogic_v10447

Let Boolean variables $p,q,r,s,t,u\in\{0,1\}$ and write
$$
\begin{aligned}
G &=\bigl[(p\oplus q)\oplus(p\to q)\bigr]\land\bigl[(s\oplus t)\to((\lnot p)\oplus r)\bigr],\\
H &=\bigl[(q\to r)\oplus(p\land u)\bigr]\land\bigl[(s\lor t)\oplus(\lnot r)\bigr],
\end{aligned}
$$
with $A\to B:=\lnot A\lor B$ and $A\oplus B:=1$ exactly when $A\neq B$.

Define
$$
M=32p+16q+8r+4s+2t+u,\qquad
N=32p+16q+8r+4s+2t+u,
$$
and set
$$
S_G=\!\!\!\sum_{\substack{G=1\\\text{bit-sum even}}}\!\!\!(7M^{2}+9),\qquad
S_H=\!\!\!\sum_{\substack{H=1\\\text{bit-sum}\ge3}}\!\!\!(5N+11).
$$

Compute
$$
\Delta=(S_G-S_H)\pmod{1009}.
$$

---

Reasoning (concise)
1.  Twelve tuples satisfy $G=1$ with an even bit-sum, giving  
    $M\in\{0,3,5,6,15,24,40,43,48,54,60,63\}$ and  
    $S_G\equiv809\pmod{1009}$.
2.  Seventeen tuples satisfy $H=1$ with bit-sum $\ge3$, giving  
    $N\in\{11,13,14,15,26,27,28,29,30,31,42,44,46,49,58,60,62\}$ and  
    $S_H\equiv85\pmod{1009}$.
3.  Hence $\Delta\equiv809-85\equiv724\pmod{1009}$.

724
